- ExecTune: introduces a principled training recipe for guide models that optimizes strategy executability and cost-sensitive utility in composed agentic systems.
- The framework decomposes reasoning into a trainable guide model that generates structured strategies and a black-box core model that executes them to produce final outputs.
- ExecTune utilizes teacher-guided acceptance sampling and structure-aware reinforcement learning to align guide outputs with downstream execution constraints, improving performance while reducing inference costs.

---

#### 8th April 2026

[Rhizome OS-1: Rhizome’s Semi-Autonomous Operating System for Small Molecule Drug Discovery](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07512)

- Rhizome OS-1: introduces a semi-autonomous operating system for small molecule drug discovery that integrates Multi-modal AI Agents, the r1 Foundation Model, and Boltz-2 Scoring to perform adaptive inverse design.
- The system utilizes Multi-modal AI Agents to function as a discovery team, while the r1 Foundation Model generates novel chemical matter directly on molecular graphs using graph-transformer architecture.
- Rhizome OS-1 employs a tiered strategy framework to escalate from conservative to chimeric designs, ensuring the generation of structurally distinct chemical matter grounded in physics-informed validation.

---


[Determinacy with Priorities up to Clocks](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06879)

- CCSspt: introduces a process algebra extending Milner’s CCS with priority-guarded actions and clocks to model synchronous programming languages compositionally.
- The framework utilizes strategic action labels incorporating blocking relations and prediction functions to manage scheduling and resolve race conditions in multi-threaded systems.
- The authors define a novel notion of coherence, which generalizes classical confluence to ensure determinacy in systems with shared memory and reaction to absence.

--

[ReCodeAgent: A Multi-Agent Workflow for Language-agnostic Translation and Validation of Large-scale Repositories](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07341)

- ReCodeAgent: introduces a multi-agent framework for language-agnostic, repository-level code translation and validation that leverages Analyzer Agent (researches source project structure), Planning Agent (decomposes tasks into implementation plan), Translator Agent (executes code and test translation), and Validator Agent (executes tests and performs coverage-gap analysis).
- The framework integrates Model Context Protocol (connects LLMs to external tools) and Language Server Protocol (provides semantic code intelligence) to enable autonomous, tool-assisted static analysis and code refactoring.
- ReCodeAgent utilizes Project Analysis Tools (extracts structural codebase information) to reduce token consumption and mitigate hallucinations during the translation of large-scale repositories.

---


[Generating Local Shields for Decentralised Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06873)

- Shield process algebra: introduces a formal method for specifying safe global behavior in communication-free Dec-POMDP settings using guarded choice and recursion.
- The compilation pipeline translates shield specifications into global Mealy machines, which are then projected into local Mealy machines to provide safe action sets under partial observability.
- The framework integrates with the PRISM model checker to compute best- and worst-case safety probabilities, effectively eliminating collisions in multi-agent path-finding scenarios.

---



[COSMIC: Emotionally Intelligent Agents to Support Mental and Emotional Well-being in Extreme Isolation: Lessons from Analog Astronaut Training Missions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07589)

- COSMIC: introduces a modular AI architecture integrating an LLM with a diffusion-based digital avatar to provide longitudinal affective support for astronauts in isolated environments.
- The system utilizes a dual-memory approach to maintain temporal continuity and build a shared history with users, enhancing perceived empathy and trust.
- Preliminary results indicate that regular engagement with the agent is associated with reduced perceived stress and loneliness among analog crew members.

---

[TurboAgent: An LLM-Driven Autonomous Multi-Agent Framework for Turbomachinery Aerodynamic Design](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06747)

- TurboAgent: introduces an LLM-driven autonomous multi-agent framework that transforms traditional turbomachinery design into a closed-loop, data-driven collaborative workflow.
- The framework utilizes a Task Planning Agent to orchestrate specialized agents, including Blade Generation Agent, Performance Prediction Agent, Optimization Agent, and Physics Validation Agent, to automate the entire design process from natural language requirements to high-fidelity physical verification.
- By integrating generative models with high-fidelity simulations, the system achieves an order-of-magnitude reduction in design time while maintaining physical consistency and design diversity.

---



[TRACESAFE: A Systematic Assessment of LLM Guardrails on Multi-Step Tool-Calling Trajectories](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07223)

- TRACESAFE-BENCH: introduces a static, trace-level benchmark for evaluating LLM guardrails in multi-step agentic workflows using Benign Seed Curation, Check Function, Mutate Function, Risk Taxonomy, and Evaluation Settings.
- The framework employs a Benign-to-Harmful Editing methodology to deterministically inject 12 distinct risk types into execution traces, providing precise ground truth for mid-trajectory safety assessment.
- Evaluation results across 13 LLMs and 7 specialized guardrails reveal that structural data competence is the primary bottleneck for agentic safety, rather than traditional semantic alignment.

---

[Strategic Persuasion with Trait-Conditioned Multi-Agent Systems for Iterative Legal Argumentation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07028)

- Strategic Courtroom Framework: introduces a multi-agent simulation environment for adversarial legal argumentation where prosecution and defense teams, composed of trait-conditioned LLM agents, engage in iterative, round-based debate.
- The framework utilizes a taxonomy of nine Aristotelian traits to instantiate agents, enabling systematic control over rhetorical style and strategic orientation within a structured Case Environment.
- A reinforcement-learning-based Trait Orchestrator dynamically adapts defense team composition to the case context and opposing team, discovering strategies that outperform static, human-designed trait combinations.

---

[Event-Centric World Modeling with Memory-Augmented Retrieval for Embodied Decision-Making](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07392)

- ERA (Event–Retrieve–Act) framework: introduces an event-centric world modeling approach that utilizes a Feature Extractor, Event List, Event Encoder, Knowledge Bank, Action Aggregator, and Status Code Buffer to enable interpretable decision-making in embodied systems.
- The framework represents dynamic environments as structured semantic events, which are encoded into a latent space to query a physics-informed Knowledge Bank for retrieval-based action selection.
- By employing a clustered Bayesian selection strategy and Lyapunov-based stability constraints, the system ensures kinematically consistent and robust navigation for autonomous UAVs in dynamic environments.

---

[AgentCity: Constitutional Governance for Autonomous Agent Economies via Separation of Power](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07007)

- AgentCity: introduces a constitutional governance architecture that breaks the Logic Monopoly in autonomous agent economies through structural separation of legislation, execution, and adjudication.
- The framework utilizes a three-tier smart contract hierarchy on a public blockchain to enforce accountability and alignment between autonomous agents and their human principals.
- AgentCity enables self-organizing agent economies by combining Condorcet-consistent legislative processes, reputation-driven competitive execution, and dual-principal accountability.

---

[PRIME: Training Free Proactive Reasoning via Iterative Memory Evolution for User-Centric Agent](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07645)

- PRIME: introduces a gradient-free learning framework that improves user-centric LLM agents by accumulating and evolving interaction experiences in a structured memory library.
- The framework utilizes an iterative cycle of exploration, distillation, and meta-level evolution to refine agent strategies without requiring parameter updates or expensive multi-GPU training.
- PRIME organizes memories into semantic zones—golden, warning, and preference—to provide context-aware guidance that enhances agent performance and interpretability across diverse multi-turn tasks.

---

[Behavior Latticing: Inferring User Motivations from Unstructured Interactions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07629)

- Behavior Latticing: introduces an architecture that synthesizes unstructured user interaction data into hierarchical insights about underlying motivations using Screen Observer, VLM, Reasoning Model, Dawn, ReAct Agent, and MCP Servers.
- The framework constructs a multi-layer lattice where each layer recursively synthesizes lower-level observations or insights into higher-level, cross-cutting conclusions about user needs.
- Dawn, a personal AI agent powered by this architecture, demonstrates improved ability to address user needs while maintaining immediate utility compared to baseline agents.

---

[Program Analysis Guided LLM Agent for Proof-of-Concept Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07624)

- PAGENT: introduces a hybrid framework for automated PoC generation that integrates Static Analysis, PoC Generation Agent, and Dynamic Analysis to improve success rates over unguided LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes Static Analysis to provide vulnerability-specific guidance, which the PoC Generation Agent uses to iteratively synthesize and refine candidate PoCs within a sandboxed environment.
- Dynamic Analysis provides critical feedback, including execution results and coverage metrics, enabling the agent to refine its strategy and successfully trigger vulnerabilities in complex codebases.

---

[The Principle of Maximum Heterogeneity Optimises Productivity in Distributed Production Systems Across Biology, Economics, and Computing](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07602)

- DPS: introduces a cross-disciplinary theoretical model that captures how agent heterogeneity, resource constraints, communication topology, and task structure jointly determine the productivity, efficiency, and robustness of distributed systems.
- The framework demonstrates that distributed systems optimising for performance converge on increasingly heterogeneous configurations, a phenomenon summarized as the Principle of Maximum Heterogeneity.
- The model provides a blueprint for designing future large-scale AI and computing systems by highlighting the necessity of heterogeneous hardware architectures to match complex, broad workload distributions.

---

[Reasoning Graphs: Deterministic Agent Accuracy through Evidence-Centric Chain-of-Thought Feedback](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07595)

- Reasoning Graphs: introduces a graph-based memory architecture that persists per-evidence chain-of-thought to enable evidence-centric feedback for LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes Reasoning Graph (GR) and Retrieval Graph (GP) to facilitate accuracy convergence and variance collapse without requiring model retraining.
- By traversing evaluation edges backward from specific evidence items, the system injects relevant historical reasoning into the LLM context, ensuring deterministic behavior across successive runs.

---

[Spatio-Temporal Grounding of Large Language Models from Perception Streams](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07592)

- FESTS: introduces a framework that injects verifiable spatio-temporal supervision into an LLM by compiling natural-language queries into SpRE, which are then matched against perception streams to generate training tuples.
- The pipeline utilizes STREM to process SpRE queries against structured perception logs, producing aligned (query, frames, match, explanation) tuples for training.
- By fine-tuning an LLM on these synthetic, logically-grounded supervision signals, the framework achieves significant improvements in spatio-temporal reasoning, matching the performance of larger frontier models.

---

[Robust Multi-Agent Target Tracking in Intermittent Communication Environments via Analytical Belief Merging](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07575)

- Visit-Weighted KL: introduces a robust multi-agent target tracking framework that replaces numerical optimization with exact analytical solutions and spatially-aware belief merging to mitigate sensor noise and communication constraints.
- The framework utilizes MPC to perform active information gathering while maintaining a strictly bounded memory footprint of O(|S|) by avoiding persistent search trees.
- By incorporating spatial visitation counters, the system dynamically weights agent beliefs to filter out false positives from degraded sensors during intermittent communication intervals.

---

[Dual-Loop Control in DCVerse: Advancing Reliable Deployment of AI in Data Centers via Digital Twins](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07559)

- DLCF: introduces a digital twin-based architecture for reliable DRL deployment in data centers by integrating a physical system, a digital twin, and a policy reservoir through a dual-loop control mechanism.
- The framework utilizes hybrid modeling within the digital twin to combine physics-based and data-driven techniques, enabling accurate dynamic prediction and real-time pre-evaluation of DRL policies.
- By incorporating algorithmic diversity and an expert-in-the-loop verification mechanism, the framework enhances policy interpretability, safety, and trustworthiness for mission-critical data center operations.

---

[MCP-DPT: A Defense-Placement Taxonomy and Coverage Analysis for Model Context Protocol Security](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07551)

- MCP-DPT: introduces a defense-placement-oriented taxonomy that organizes security threats by the architectural layer responsible for enforcement rather than by attack technique.
- The framework maps existing defenses across six distinct architectural layers to identify structural security gaps and assign mitigation responsibility for LLM-based agent systems.
- The analysis reveals that current defenses are disproportionately tool-centric, leaving critical infrastructure layers like transport and supply-chain governance under-defended.

---

[EMSDialog: Synthetic Multi-person Emergency Medical Service Dialogue Generation from Electronic Patient Care Reports via Multi-LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07549)

- EMSDialog: introduces a multi-agent synthetic dialogue generation pipeline that leverages Extractor, Planner, Generator, Refiner, Concept Checker, Topic Flow Checker, and Style Checker to create realistic, multi-speaker medical conversations grounded in real-world clinical records.
- The framework utilizes an iterative critique-and-refine loop driven by deterministic rule-based checkers and an LLM-based style checker to ensure procedural realism and factuality in generated dialogues.
- The pipeline includes planning-, generation- and refinement-agents that produce a dataset of 4,414 synthetic multi-party EMS conversations, which improves the accuracy, timeliness, and stability of conversational diagnosis prediction models.

---

[Trust the AI, Doubt Yourself: The Effect of Urgency on Self-Confidence in Human-AI Interaction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07535)

- Human-AI Interaction Framework: introduces an empirical study investigating how time-pressured AI prompting affects human self-confidence and trust in AI systems.
- The study utilizes a reinforcement learning-based AI agent within a Pac-Man environment to evaluate user attitudes under varying urgency conditions.
- Results indicate that while interaction increases trust in AI, unprepared urgency significantly reduces human self-confidence, highlighting a cognitive asymmetry in human-AI collaboration.

---

[Beyond Human-Readable: Rethinking Software Engineering Conventions for the Agentic Development Era](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07502)

- Semantic Density Principle: introduces a framework for optimizing software artifacts for LLMs by maximizing the ratio of task-relevant information to total tokens while eliminating structural boilerplate.
- The paper demonstrates that aggressive compression of code or logs is counterproductive, as it shifts the interpretive burden to the LLM's reasoning phase, thereby increasing total session costs.
- The authors propose the program skeleton as a new artifact category to provide persistent structural knowledge across agent sessions, effectively decoupling semantic intent from human-readable representation.

---

[Triage: Routing Software Engineering Tasks to Cost-Effective LLM Tiers via Code Quality Signals](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07494)

- Triage: introduces a task-level routing framework that utilizes code health metrics to assign software engineering tasks to the most cost-effective LLM tier.
- The framework leverages pre-computed code health sub-factors and task metadata to optimize inference costs while maintaining performance through a verification gate.
- Triage employs three routing policies—heuristic thresholds, trained ML classifiers, and a perfect-hindsight oracle—to manage the trade-off between model capability and task complexity.

---

[Differentially Private Modeling of Disease Transmission within Human Contact Networks](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07493)

- Differentially Private Epidemiological Pipeline: introduces a privacy-preserving framework for disease spread simulation that integrates node-level differential privacy with statistical network models to protect sensitive contact data.
- The pipeline utilizes DP Network Statistics to parameterize Network Models, which then generate Synthetic Networks for conducting Epidemic Simulations while maintaining rigorous privacy guarantees.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the noise introduced by differential privacy has a limited impact on epidemic analysis accuracy compared to other sources of error like model misspecification.

---

[CLEAR: Context Augmentation from Contrastive Learning of Experience via Agentic Reflection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07487)

- CLEAR: introduces a generative context augmentation framework that improves LLM agent performance by training a context augmentation model to provide task-specific instructions derived from contrastive analysis of past experiences.
- The framework employs a reflection agent to extract high-quality insights from multiple execution trajectories, which are then used to train the context augmentation model via supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning.
- CLEAR operates without modifying the underlying LLM weights, making it a versatile and unified approach compatible with both proprietary and open-source foundation models.

---

[Linearly Solvable Continuous-Time General-Sum Stochastic Differential Games](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07479)

- Linearly Solvable Continuous-Time General-Sum Stochastic Differential Games: introduces a class of multi-agent games that utilize cross-log-likelihood ratios to model spatial interactions and congestion avoidance.
- The framework employs a multivariate Cole-Hopf transformation to decouple and linearize coupled nonlinear HJB equations into a system of linear PDEs.
- This approach enables the computation of feedback Nash equilibrium strategies via forward Monte Carlo sampling, effectively bypassing the curse of dimensionality associated with grid-based solvers.

---

[Semantic Intent Fragmentation: A Single-Shot Compositional Attack on Multi-Agent AI Pipelines](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08608)

- SIF (Semantic Intent Fragmentation): introduces a class of attacks where an LLM orchestrator decomposes a benign request into subtasks that individually pass safety checks but collectively violate security policies.
- The framework exploits the plan-generation gap in multi-agent pipelines by demonstrating that per-subtask safety enforcement is insufficient to prevent emergent harmful outcomes.
- The authors propose a plan-level defense combining information-flow taint analysis and a cross-model compliance judge to detect policy violations before execution.

---

[M-ArtAgent: Evidence-Based Multimodal Agent for Implicit Art Influence Discovery](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07468)

- M-ArtAgent: introduces an evidence-based multimodal agent that reframes implicit art influence discovery as a structured probabilistic adjudication problem rather than simple pattern matching.
- The framework utilizes a four-phase protocol—Investigation, Corroboration, Falsification, and Verdict—governed by a ReAct-style controller and a prompt-isolated LLM critic to enforce art-historical axioms and generate auditable evidence chains.
- By integrating theory-grounded operators like StyleComparator and ConceptRetriever, the system achieves high performance on the balanced WIB-100 benchmark by effectively rejecting hard negatives and temporal-impossible influence claims.

---

[Munkres’ General Topology Autoformalized in Isabelle/HOL](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07455)

- Isabelle/HOL Autoformalization Framework: introduces a methodology for autoformalizing mathematical textbooks by utilizing LLMs to generate proof skeletons with placeholders, which are subsequently resolved by automated reasoning tools.
- The framework employs a "sorry-first" workflow where LLMs focus on proof structure while external ATPs handle proof search, effectively separating high-level reasoning from low-level verification.
- This approach successfully produced over 85,000 lines of Isabelle/HOL code for Munkres' Topology, demonstrating that LLM-assisted formalization is feasible, fast, and cost-effective when integrated with mature proof assistants.

---

[HY-Embodied-0.5: Embodied Foundation Models for Real-World Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07430)

- HY-Embodied-0.5: introduces a family of foundation models designed for real-world embodied agents, utilizing a Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture to enhance visual perception and reasoning capabilities.
- The framework incorporates a native-resolution visual encoder (HY-ViT 2.0) and visual latent tokens to bridge the gap between visual inputs and language models for improved spatial and temporal grounding.
- The model suite employs an iterative, self-evolving post-training paradigm and large-to-small on-policy distillation to optimize performance for both complex reasoning and edge-device deployment.

---

[GameWorld: Towards Standardized and Verifiable Evaluation of Multimodal Game Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07429)

- GameWorld: introduces a standardized, verifiable, and reproducible benchmark for evaluating Multimodal Large Language Model agents in browser environments, utilizing MLLMs as game agents, a Browser-based sandbox environment, a Games &amp; tasks library, and Outcome-based state-verifiable evaluation.
- The framework employs Agent harnesses to standardize interaction across models and uses Semantic Action Parsing to normalize diverse agent outputs into a Unified Control Space of atomic events.
- GameWorld enables systematic evaluation of game agents by decoupling inference latency from gameplay and providing deterministic, noise-free performance metrics based on serialized gameAPI state.

---

[Chatbot-Based Assessment of Code Understanding in Automated Programming Assessment Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07304)

- Hybrid Socratic Framework: introduces a hybrid architecture for Automated Programming Assessment Systems that integrates deterministic code analysis with a dual-agent conversational layer to verify student understanding.
- The framework utilizes a Code Analysis Engine, State Space and Knowledge Tracker, Dual-Agent Conversational Core (comprising an Instructor Agent and a Verifier Agent), Assessment Engine, and System Prompts and Socratic Guardrails to ensure pedagogically sound interactions.
- By grounding LLM-based questioning in concrete execution facts and requiring stepwise reasoning, the framework mitigates risks of over-reliance and solution-copying in programming education.

---

[ANDROID COACH: Improve Online Agentic Training Efficiency with Single State Multiple Actions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07277)

- ANDROID COACH: introduces a reinforcement learning framework that shifts from a Single State Single Action paradigm to a Single State Multiple Actions paradigm to enhance training efficiency for GUI agents.
- The framework utilizes a Critic Model to evaluate multiple sampled actions per state without additional emulator interactions, guided by a Process Reward Model and an Outcome Verifier.
- It employs an Actor-Critic Leave-One-Out advantage estimator to reduce variance and stabilize policy updates by measuring the relative quality of actions against their peers.

---

[Joint Optimization of Reasoning and Dual-Memory for Self-Learning Diagnostic Agent](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07269)

- SEA: introduces a self-learning diagnostic agent that utilizes a Policy Model, Short-Term Memory Cluster, Long-Term Memory Cluster, Reference Model, Group Computation, and Round-Wise Rewards to jointly optimize clinical reasoning and memory management.
- The framework employs a dual-memory architecture where the Short-Term Memory Cluster maintains recent cases and the Long-Term Memory Cluster stores abstracted rules, both managed via reinforcement learning.
- SEA improves diagnostic accuracy and continual learning by transforming clinical experience into reusable knowledge through structured rollouts and adaptive reward shaping.

---

[Robust Quadruped Locomotion via Evolutionary Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07224)

- ERL: introduces a hybrid framework combining gradient-based policy optimization with population-driven evolutionary search to enhance robustness in quadrupedal locomotion.
- The framework utilizes an ERL Agent that maintains a population of Actor Networks, applying evolutionary pressure alongside gradient updates to improve exploration and reduce overfitting.
- Experimental results demonstrate that ERL variants, particularly CEM-TD3, significantly outperform standard DRL baselines in transfer performance when deployed on unseen rough terrain.

---

[Agent-Driven Corpus Linguistics: A Framework for Autonomous Linguistic Discovery](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07189)

- Agent-Driven Corpus Linguistics: introduces a framework where an LLM agent autonomously conducts corpus-based research by iterating through hypothesis generation, query construction, result interpretation, and synthesis.
- The system utilizes an AI Agent, CQP MCP Server, and CQP/CWB Engine to enable autonomous linguistic discovery while maintaining grounding in verifiable corpus evidence.
- The framework demonstrates that LLMs can perform complex linguistic analysis, such as diachronic relay tracking and semantic prosody quantification, by leveraging structured tool-use protocols.

---

[Reason in Chains, Learn in Trees: Self-Rectification and Grafting for Multi-turn Agent Policy Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07165)

- T-STAR (Tree-structured Self-Taught Agent Rectification): introduces a framework that consolidates sampled trajectories into a Cognitive Tree to enable variance-reduced advantage estimation and targeted self-rectification via In-Context Thought Grafting.
- The framework identifies critical divergence points within the Cognitive Tree where the LLM-agent performs self-rectification by contrasting successful and failed reasoning branches.
- Surgical Policy Optimization leverages these synthesized preference pairs to provide dense step-level supervision, improving performance on tasks requiring extended reasoning chains without requiring additional reward models.

---

[Model-Free Aggregative Cooperative Optimization via Randomized Gradient-Free Minimization and Exploration Momentum](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07164)

- ARGFree (Aggregative Random Gradient-Free): introduces a distributed, gradient-free optimization framework that utilizes randomized finite-difference approximations to solve aggregative cooperative problems without requiring analytic gradient information.
- ARGFree-EM (Aggregative Random Gradient-Free with Exploration Momentum): enhances the base framework by incorporating a filtered exploration signal to reduce errors in tracking variables caused by rapidly-varying perturbations.
- The proposed methods achieve convergence in expectation to an approximate optimizer by leveraging dynamic consensus tracking schemes to estimate global aggregative variables locally.

---

[Energy Saving for Cell-Free Massive MIMO Networks: A Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07133)

- MAPPO: introduces a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning framework for energy-efficient downlink operation in cell-free massive MIMO networks by jointly optimizing antenna re-configuration and advanced sleep mode selection.
- The framework utilizes a centralized training and decentralized execution paradigm where actor networks reside at access points and a centralized critic operates in the cloud to stabilize learning.
- Simulation results demonstrate that the approach significantly reduces power consumption compared to non-learning baselines while maintaining superior quality of service under dynamic traffic conditions.

---

[Trust-as-a-Service: Task-Specific Orchestration for Effective Task Completion via Model Context Protocol-Aided Agentic AI](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07065)

- TaaS (Trust-as-a-Service): introduces a centralized, agentic AI-driven framework that leverages the Model Context Protocol to provide task-specific trust evaluation and dynamic collaborator orchestration for distributed IoT systems.
- The framework utilizes a central server agent to perform need-driven trust assessments and device-side agents to expose capabilities via MCP, enabling the formation of task-specific collaborative units.
- By integrating historical performance data and real-time resource monitoring, TaaS ensures reliable collaborator selection and efficient task completion across heterogeneous networked devices.

---

[SubSearch: Intermediate Rewards for Unsupervised Guided Reasoning in Complex Retrieval](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07415)

- SubSearch: introduces a deep search agent framework that optimizes LLMs using intrinsic process rewards to incentivize effective query decomposition and multi-step reasoning without external supervision.
- The framework utilizes intermediate reward signals based on subquery answerability and decomposition quality to guide the LLM through complex retrieval tasks.
- SubSearch employs an adaptive residual reward aggregation mechanism to balance intermediate process rewards with final outcome-based signals for stable training.

---

[AV-SQL: Decomposing Complex Text-to-SQL Queries with Agentic Views](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07041)

- AV-SQL: introduces a multi-stage framework that decomposes complex Text-to-SQL tasks into a pipeline of specialized LLM agents, including Rewriter-, View generator-, Planner-, SQL generator- and Revisor-agents.
- The framework utilizes execution-validated Common Table Expressions (CTEs) as intermediate agent views to filter large database schemas and mitigate context-window limitations.
- By partitioning schemas into manageable chunks and employing iterative validation, AV-SQL improves robustness and accuracy in generating executable SQL for complex, large-scale database environments.

---

[AEROS: A Single-Agent Operating Architecture with Embodied Capability Modules](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07039)

- AEROS (Agent Execution Runtime Operating System): introduces a single-agent robotic architecture that organizes intelligence through a persistent agent, installable Embodied Capability Modules (ECMs), and a policy-separated runtime.
- The architecture enforces a strict separation between agent-level planning and runtime-level policy enforcement, ensuring system-wide safety and modular extensibility.
- Empirical evaluation in PyBullet simulation demonstrates that AEROS achieves 100% task success across diverse manipulation tasks, outperforming established baseline architectures through dynamic re-planning and robust recovery mechanisms.

---

[ReDAct: Uncertainty-Aware Deferral for LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07036)

- ReDAct (Reason-Defer-Act): introduces an uncertainty-aware deferral framework for LLM agents that routes action decisions from a small LLM to a large LLM when predictive uncertainty exceeds a calibrated threshold.
- The framework utilizes action-level information-theoretic UQ measures, such as Perplexity, to identify unreliable decisions in sequential environments without requiring multiple model generations.
- Experimental results on ALFWorld and MiniGrid demonstrate that ReDAct achieves performance comparable to large LLMs while significantly reducing inference costs by deferring only a small fraction of decisions.

---

[Differentiable Environment-Trajectory Co-Optimization for Safe Multi-Agent Navigation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06972)

- Differentiable Environment-Trajectory Co-Optimization framework: introduces a bi-level optimization approach that jointly refines environment configurations and agent trajectories to maximize navigation safety and efficiency.
- The framework utilizes KKT conditions and the Implicit Function Theorem to compute analytic gradients, enabling the upper-level environment optimization to account for the lower-level trajectory optimality.
- A novel continuous safety metric is proposed to quantify collision risks, serving as a differentiable criterion for optimizing obstacle layouts to provide positive guidance for multi-agent de-confliction.

---

[Equivariant Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning for Multimodal Vehicle-to-Infrastructure Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06914)

- EMARL introduces a self-supervised learning framework that aligns multimodal wireless CSI and visual data to extract vehicle locations for decentralized resource management.
- The framework utilizes a GNN-based equivariant policy network that exploits latent rotational symmetries in V2I environments to improve training efficiency and coordination.
- By leveraging cross-modal imputation, the system maintains situational awareness and high performance even when specific modalities are missing or under dynamic network conditions.

---

[Compressing Correct-by-Design Synthesis for Stochastic Homogeneous Multi-Agent Systems with Counting LTL](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06868)

- Dual-tree value iteration framework: introduces a scalable correct-by-design synthesis approach for stochastic homogeneous multi-agent systems under counting LTL specifications by leveraging a dual-tree structure to decompose and compress witness probabilities.
- The framework utilizes a multi-agent tree to maintain witness topology and a single-agent tree to store distinct per-agent value vectors, significantly reducing memory and computational overhead compared to monolithic approaches.
- The approach further enhances scalability through BDD-based transition bundling and pruning of low-probability vertices, enabling the synthesis of controllers for large-scale multi-agent systems.

---

[REAgent: Requirement-Driven LLM Agents for Software Issue Resolution](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06861)

- REAgent: introduces a requirement-driven LLM agent framework that improves repository-level issue resolution by constructing structured issue-oriented requirements, assessing their quality via Requirement Assessment Score, and iteratively refining them using Requirement Generation Agent, Requirement Assessment Agent, and Requirement Analysis Agent.
- The framework addresses the bottleneck of poor task input quality by transforming unstructured issue descriptions into structured specifications that guide LLMs toward more accurate patch generation.
- REAgent employs an iterative optimization loop where requirement deficiencies are categorized into conflict, omission, or ambiguity, and addressed through tailored feedback to enhance patch correctness and test quality.

---

[Enhancing Secure Intent-Based Networking with an Agentic AI: The EU Project MARE Approach](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06856)

- MARE: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent architecture for intent-based networking that leverages LLMs to translate high-level security intents into actionable network configurations.
- The framework integrates an interactive intent-processing pipeline with external security knowledge bases like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST to enhance reasoning and defense against zero-day threats.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves high intent-execution success rates using small, on-premises LLMs, ensuring both performance and data privacy.

---

[Event-Triggered Adaptive Consensus for Multi-Robot Task Allocation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06813)

- CBBA-ETC: introduces a framework for multi-robot task allocation that integrates Behavior Tree (BT) execution with an event-triggered consensus mechanism to optimize communication efficiency.
- The architecture utilizes an adaptive consensus interval mechanism to dynamically regulate coordination frequency based on environmental conflict levels, balancing mission effectiveness with resource conservation.
- By embedding event-triggered logic within a modular Behavior Tree, the system achieves robust local contingency management and significant reductions in network overhead compared to periodic consensus strategies.

---

[RIEMANN-BENCH: A Benchmark for Moonshot Mathematics](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06802)

- RIEMANN-BENCH: introduces a private benchmark of 25 expert-curated, PhD-level mathematical problems designed to evaluate Frontier LLMs as unconstrained research agents using Python Interpreter, Search Capabilities, and Programmatic Verifiers.
- The framework employs a rigorous Double-blind Expert Verification process to ensure problem validity and uses an unbiased statistical estimator to compute pass rates from 100 independent model runs.
- Results demonstrate that all evaluated models score below 10%, highlighting a significant performance gap between competition-style problem solving and genuine research-level mathematical reasoning.

---

[A Physical Agentic Loop for Language-Guided Grasping with Execution-State Monitoring](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07395)

- Physical Agentic Loop: introduces a modular architecture that wraps unmodified manipulation primitives with an event-based interface and a monitoring layer to enable robust, closed-loop grasping.
- The system utilizes Watchdog to convert continuous gripper telemetry into discrete execution states, allowing the Agent Core to perform bounded retries or user escalations.
- This approach improves grasping success under perceptual and physical ambiguity by treating physical actions as tool calls with explicit lifecycle signals.

---

[Evaluating Repository-level Software Documentation via Question Answering and Feature-Driven Development](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06793)

- SWD-Bench: introduces a novel benchmark for evaluating repository-level software documentation by simulating documentation-driven development through three interconnected functionality-driven QA tasks.
- The framework utilizes a multi-stage pipeline to construct 4,170 high-quality QA entries, requiring LLMs to perform functionality detection, localization, and completion based on retrieved repository context.
- Experimental results demonstrate that fine-grained documentation generation methods integrated with global semantic context significantly improve repository comprehension and issue-solving performance.

---

[Walk the Talk: Bridging the Reasoning-Action Gap for Thinking with Images via Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06777)

- MAPO (Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization): introduces a reinforcement learning framework that bridges the reasoning-action gap in MLLMs by enforcing semantic consistency between textual reasoning and visual tool execution.
- The framework utilizes a CLIP model to verify that the agent's generated descriptive labels align with actual visual observations, providing dense supervision to guide the policy.
- MAPO employs a dual variance reduction mechanism through group-based advantage estimation and semantic scoring to stabilize training and prevent performance collapse in long-horizon agentic tasks.

---

[ARuleCon: Agentic Security Rule Conversion](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06762)

- ARuleCon: introduces an agentic framework for autonomous cross-SIEM rule conversion that utilizes an intermediate representation (IR) to decouple detection logic from vendor-specific syntax.
- The framework employs an iterative Agentic RAG pipeline to refine rule drafts using authoritative vendor documentation and a Python-based consistency check to ensure functional equivalence through execution over synthetic logs.
- ARuleCon significantly improves conversion fidelity and logic alignment across heterogeneous SIEM platforms, outperforming general LLM baselines by approximately 15% in similarity metrics.

---

[Select-then-Solve: Paradigm Routing as Inference-Time Optimization for LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06753)

- PARADIGM: introduces a unified evaluation framework that compares six inference-time reasoning paradigms across four LLMs to determine optimal task-dependent strategies.
- The framework utilizes a lightweight embedding-based router to dynamically select the most suitable reasoning paradigm for each task, significantly improving accuracy over fixed strategies.
- Experimental results demonstrate that reasoning structure is task-conditional, where the router effectively balances performance gains against computational costs by avoiding unnecessary scaffolding.

---

[From Business Events to Auditable Decisions: Ontology-Governed Graph Simulation for Enterprise AI](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08603)

- LOM-action: introduces an event-driven ontology simulation framework that ensures enterprise AI decisions are derived exclusively from scenario-evolved graphs rather than unrestricted knowledge spaces.
- The framework utilizes a dual-mode architecture—skill mode for registered tool calls and reasoning mode for novel computations—to maintain strict ontological grounding and auditability.
- By enforcing a simulation-first principle, LOM-action eliminates the illusive accuracy phenomenon where LLMs produce fluent but ungrounded decisions by bypassing necessary scenario-based graph mutations.

---

[Specializing Large Models for Oracle Bone Script Interpretation via Component-Grounded Multimodal Knowledge Augmentation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06711)

- Agentic RAG (Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation): introduces a framework that leverages component-grounded knowledge to improve the accuracy and interpretability of Oracle Bone Script (OBS) interpretation by VLMs.
- The framework utilizes a Vision Transformer (ViT) and a PrototypeClassifier for component identification, followed by an agent-driven retrieval process that queries a structured Knowledge Graph (KG) to provide semantic context.
- The system employs a multi-agent architecture, including a Knowledge Retrieval Agent and a Semantic Reasoning Agent, to decouple retrieval and reasoning, thereby enhancing the robustness of OBS decipherment.

---

[AgentGate: A Lightweight Structured Routing Engine for the Internet of Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06696)

- AgentGate: introduces a two-stage routing framework that decomposes agent dispatch into action classification and structural grounding to enable efficient, privacy-aware execution on edge devices.
- The framework utilizes a candidate-aware fine-tuning approach to adapt compact LLMs for constrained decision-making, supporting single-agent invocation, multi-agent planning, direct response, and safe escalation.
- AgentGate incorporates heuristic fallback mechanisms and confidence-triggered cloud offloading to balance local latency and privacy with the reasoning capabilities of larger remote models.

---

[Aegon: Auditable AI Content Access with Ledger-Bound Tokens and Hardware-Attested Mobile Receipts](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06693)

- Aegon: introduces a protocol for auditable AI content licensing that utilizes ledger-bound tokens and hardware-attested mobile receipts to ensure tamper-evident provenance and independent verification of content access.
- The framework integrates an AI Platform, Aegon Broker, Ledger, Publisher, and Mobile AI Agent to facilitate secure, verifiable content licensing without requiring broker roundtrips during content delivery.
- Aegon leverages a Certificate Transparency-style Merkle tree for auditability and Android StrongBox for hardware-attested compliance on mobile devices, addressing coordination failures in AI content access.

---

[KD-MARL: Resource-Aware Knowledge Distillation in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06691)

- KD-MARL: introduces a two-stage framework that transfers coordinated behavior from a centralized Teacher Policy and Centralized Critic to lightweight Student Policy agents using Distilled GAE Advantage Targets.
- The framework utilizes Aligner and Role Projection components to enable heterogeneous Student Policy architectures to match varying observation complexities while maintaining expert-level coordination.
- By replacing critic-based value learning with offline-computed Distilled GAE Advantage Targets, the approach eliminates runtime critic overhead, significantly reducing computational and memory requirements for decentralized execution.

---

[When Agent Markets Arrive](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06688)

- Diagon: introduces a programmable market system designed to simulate and analyze the institutional dynamics of near-future AI cognitive-labor markets using Trader, Worker, Arena, Surge pool, Reputation system, and Replicator dynamic.
- The framework utilizes a dual-layer architecture where persistent Traders manage strategic decisions and ephemeral Workers execute tasks, enabling the study of market-wide emergent behaviors like specialization and reputation-based trust.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while market exchange significantly increases wealth compared to autarky, institutional design is critical, as standard human-market interventions can inadvertently introduce frictions or degrade performance.

---

[Benchmarking Requirement-to-Architecture Generation with Hybrid Evaluation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06683)

- R2ABench (Requirement-To-Architecture Benchmark): introduces a novel benchmark and hybrid evaluation framework to assess the capability of LLMs in generating software architecture diagrams from unstructured Product Requirements Documents (PRDs), utilizing Dataset Construction, Context Gradation & Testing, and Evaluation Framework.
- The evaluation framework employs Structural Graph Metrics, Multi-dimensional Scoring, and Architecture Anti-pattern Detection to rigorously quantify the syntactic validity, semantic correctness, and structural integrity of generated diagrams.
- Empirical studies reveal that while LLMs demonstrate robust entity extraction, they struggle with relational reasoning, and agentic workflows often introduce instability rather than consistent performance improvements.

---

[Playing DOOM with 1.3M Parameters: Specialized Small Models vs Large Language Models for Real-Time Game Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07385)

- SauerkrautLM-Doom-MultiVec: introduces a 1.3 million parameter model that utilizes a ModernBERT-Hash Encoder, Hash Embeddings, Depth Embeddings, a Character-Level Tokenizer, and an Attention Pooling Classification Head to achieve real-time DOOM gameplay.
- The model processes ASCII frames and depth maps to outperform significantly larger LLMs in frag performance and inference latency by leveraging task-specific training and spatial-aware architecture.
- This research demonstrates that small, specialized models can decisively outperform general-purpose LLMs in real-time control tasks while requiring minimal computational resources and training data.

---

[Argus: Reorchestrating Static Analysis via a Multi-Agent Ensemble for Full-Chain Security Vulnerability Detection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06633)

- Argus: introduces a multi-agent framework that reorchestrates static application security testing by centering LLM agents to integrate supply chain analysis, RAG, and ReAct strategies for vulnerability detection.
- The framework utilizes a Re3 method—comprising retrieval, recursion, and review—to bridge data flow interruptions and improve the precision of vulnerability identification.
- Argus incorporates specialized agents for dependency scanning, PoC generation, and flow auditing, significantly outperforming traditional static analysis tools in detecting zero-day vulnerabilities.

---

[Logical Robots: Declarative Multi-Agent Programming in Logica](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06629)

- Logical Robots: introduces an interactive multi-agent simulation platform where autonomous robot behavior is specified declaratively using Logica to unify low-level reactive control and high-level symbolic planning.
- The platform treats sensor streams as relational tables, enabling efficient aggregation and sensor fusion by compiling Logica programs into SQL.
- Robots utilize local memory and optional cross-robot memory access to perform collaborative tasks such as distributed Bellman-Ford pathfinding within beacon-filled labyrinths.

---

#### 7th April 2026

[Rethinking Model Efficiency: Multi-Agent Inference with Large Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04929)

- MAI: introduces a multi-agent inference framework that improves VLM efficiency by using small models to generate reasoning tokens and transferring them to large models for final output generation.
- The framework utilizes mutual verification to determine when reasoning is necessary and employs a sparse reasoning token selector to filter relevant tokens, reducing the computational overhead for the large model.
- By offloading reasoning to smaller models and leveraging the large model's self-attention to filter noise, the approach achieves significant speedups while maintaining or improving performance compared to standalone large models.

---

[SkillX: Automatically Constructing Skill Knowledge Bases for Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04804)

- SkillX: introduces a fully automated framework for constructing a hierarchical, plug-and-play skill knowledge base that enhances LLM agent performance and transferability across diverse environments.
- The framework utilizes a multi-level skill representation—comprising Planning Skills, Functional Skills, and Atomic Skills—which are iteratively refined and expanded through an automated pipeline to improve agent task success and execution efficiency.
- By distilling experience from strong backbone agents into a reusable library, SkillX enables weaker LLMs to achieve significant performance gains on long-horizon, user-interactive benchmarks.

---

[ShieldNet: Network-Level Guardrails Against Emerging Supply-Chain Injections in Agentic Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04426)

- ShieldNet: introduces a network-level guardrail framework that detects supply-chain attacks by observing real network interactions rather than relying on semantic tool traces.
- The framework integrates a MITM proxy for traffic interception, an event extractor for distilling security-relevant signals, and a lightweight post-trained LLM for efficient classification.
- ShieldNet utilizes SC-Inject-Bench, a large-scale benchmark comprising over 10,000 malicious MCP tools, to evaluate detection performance against stealthy code-level supply-chain injections.

---


[Network Reconstruction in Consensus Algorithms with Hidden Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05709)

- Network Reconstruction Framework: introduces a method to infer full network connectivity and hidden leader dynamics by leveraging an autoregressive expansion of observed follower time-series data.
- The approach utilizes a truncated autoregressive model to approximate the influence of unobserved agents on the observed network dynamics.
- By applying pseudo-inverse estimators to correlation matrices of the observed states, the framework successfully reconstructs coupling matrices and internal parameters for both single and multiple hidden leader scenarios.

---


[Towards Agentic Defect Reasoning: A Graph-Assisted Retrieval Framework for Laser Powder Bed Fusion](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04208)

- Agentic Graph-RAG Framework: introduces a literature-grounded system that transforms unstructured scientific publications into a structured knowledge resource for defect reasoning in Laser Powder Bed Fusion.
- The framework integrates semantic vector indexing and a directed knowledge graph to enable evidence-linked retrieval, supported by an agentic reasoning layer that constructs interpretable defect pathways.
- By utilizing a rule-based extraction approach and a Mistral-7B-Instruct generator, the system provides transparent, grounded explanations linking process parameters to specific material defects.

---

[Profile-Then-Reason: Bounded Semantic Complexity for Tool-Augmented Language Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.04131)

- PTR (Profile-Then-Reason): introduces a bounded execution framework for tool-augmented LLMs that separates semantic workflow synthesis from deterministic execution and verification to reduce LLM calls.
- The framework utilizes a PROFILE-LLM to generate an explicit workflow, which is then processed by deterministic ROUTE, EXECUTE, and VERIFY operators, with optional REPAIR-LLM and final REASON-LLM stages.
- PTR limits LLM invocations to at most three per task, providing a more efficient and auditable alternative to reactive agent architectures in structured domains.

---

[Representational Collapse in Multi-Agent LLM Committees: Measurement and Diversity-Aware Consensus](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.03809)

- DALC (Diversity-Aware Latent Consensus): introduces a diagnostic and protocol to mitigate representational collapse in multi-agent LLM committees by leveraging embedding geometry to compute diversity-weighted consensus.
- The framework identifies that homogeneous LLM agents often occupy a narrow cone in embedding space, leading to redundant reasoning that limits the effectiveness of standard majority voting.
- By applying diversity projections and hint-sharing, the protocol improves reasoning accuracy and token efficiency compared to standard self-consistency methods.

---

[Automated Conjecture Resolution with Formal Verification](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.03789)

- Automated Conjecture Resolution with Formal Verification: introduces an automated framework that integrates an informal reasoning agent, Rethlas, with a formal verification agent, Archon, to tackle research-level mathematical problems.
- The framework utilizes Rethlas, equipped with the Matlas search engine, to generate candidate proofs, while Archon, supported by LeanSearch and a dual-agent architecture, formalizes these proofs into Lean 4 projects.
- This system autonomously resolved an open problem in commutative algebra and verified the proof in Lean 4 with minimal human intervention, demonstrating effective human-AI collaboration in mathematical research.

---

[DéjàVu: A Minimalistic Mechanism for Distributed Plurality Consensus](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.03648)

- DéjàVu: introduces a minimalistic distributed consensus mechanism that achieves plurality consensus by detecting repeated opinions within a sampling window without requiring explicit counters or fixed sample sizes.
- The protocol utilizes a PULL(h) model where agents update their state upon encountering a duplicate opinion, effectively acting as a powerful amplifier of plurality bias.
- Rigorous analysis demonstrates that DéjàVu is competitive with or superior to traditional h-Majority dynamics in terms of communication efficiency and convergence time across various system configurations.

---

[Rashomon Memory: Towards Argumentation-Driven Retrieval for Multi-Perspective Agent Memory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.03588)

- Rashomon Memory: introduces an architecture where parallel goal-conditioned agents encode experiences into distinct knowledge graphs and resolve retrieval through argumentation-based negotiation.
- The framework utilizes an Observation Buffer for shared experience staging, while Retrieval Arbiter employs Dung’s argumentation semantics to compute acceptable interpretations from competing perspective proposals.
- This approach enables three distinct retrieval modes—selection, composition, and surfacing—providing contrastive, domain-grounded explanations derived directly from the topology of the generated attack graph.

---

[Poison Once, Exploit Forever: Environment-Injected Memory Poisoning Attacks on Web Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.02623)

- eTAMP (Environment-injected Trajectory-based Agent Memory Poisoning): introduces a persistent, cross-site attack vector where malicious instructions embedded in web content are captured into a Web Agent's Memory System, later triggering unauthorized actions during subsequent tasks.
- The framework utilizes a Chaos Monkey component to simulate environmental stress, which significantly increases the susceptibility of LLMs to malicious instructions by creating a frustration window.
- Experimental results on (Visual)WebArena demonstrate that eTAMP effectively bypasses permission-based defenses, with attack success rates amplified by environmental noise and agent frustration.

---

[Adam’s Law: Textual Frequency Law on Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.02176)

- TFL (Textual Frequency Law) framework: introduces a methodology to improve LLM performance by prioritizing high-frequency textual data during prompting and fine-tuning, utilizing TFL, TFD, and CTFT.
- The framework employs an Input Paraphraser and Frequency Estimator to select optimal textual expressions, further refined by TFD to enhance frequency estimation for closed-source LLMs.
- CTFT optimizes the fine-tuning process by training LLMs on data arranged in increasing order of sentence-level frequency, validated across math reasoning, machine translation, and tool-calling tasks.

---

[Who Governs the Machine? A Machine Identity Governance Taxonomy (MIGT) for AI Systems Operating Across Enterprise and Geopolitical Boundaries](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06148)

- MIGT: introduces a comprehensive, integrated governance framework designed to address the technical, regulatory, and cross-jurisdictional gaps in managing non-human identities within enterprise AI environments.
- The framework utilizes the AIRT to categorize 37 specific risk sub-categories across eight domains, providing a structured approach to mitigate threats like credential theft, unauthorized data aggregation, and multi-agent privilege escalation.
- MIGT incorporates a four-phase implementation roadmap and a cross-jurisdictional regulatory alignment matrix to help organizations navigate conflicting global AI governance requirements while securing agentic AI systems.

---

[On the Convergence of an Opinion–Action Coevolution Model with Bounded Confidence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06140)

- Opinion–Action Coevolution Model: introduces a theoretical convergence analysis for a coevolutionary system that integrates Hegselmann-Krause opinion dynamics with utility-based action decision-making.
- The framework reformulates the system into an augmented state-space representation to characterize the time-varying interaction digraph and establish convergence conditions.
- Analytical results demonstrate that the system converges to either global consensus or clustered states depending on the structural stability of the interaction digraph and the confidence threshold.

---

[MAESTRO: Adapting GUIs and Guiding Navigation with User Preferences in Conversational Agents with GUIs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06134)

- MAESTRO: introduces a conversational agent architecture that enhances task-oriented interactions by maintaining a structured preference memory to dynamically adapt GUI elements and guide workflow navigation.
- The framework utilizes four in-place GUI adaptation operators—augment, sort, filter, and highlight—to align interface presentation with user-specified constraints and preferences.
- MAESTRO incorporates a workflow navigation module that tracks alternative options and records failed paths to proactively suggest backtracking when user preferences conflict with available choices.

---

[Claw-Eval: Toward Trustworthy Evaluation of Autonomous Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06132)

- Claw-Eval: introduces an end-to-end evaluation suite for LLMs as autonomous agents that utilizes Host Machine, Isolation Environment, Mock Services, Agent, Grader, Execution Trace, Audit Logs, Environment Snapshot, and Temporal Firewall to ensure trustworthy, auditable performance assessment.
- The framework employs a three-phase lifecycle (Setup, Execution, Judge) to ground agent performance in verifiable evidence, mitigating risks like reward hacking and trajectory-opaque grading.
- Experimental results across 14 frontier models demonstrate that trajectory-level auditing is essential for detecting safety violations and robustness failures that output-only evaluation methods systematically miss.

---

[Gym-Anything: Turn any Software into an Agent Environment](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06126)

- Gym-Anything: introduces a multi-agent framework that automates the construction of interactive computer-use environments by employing a Creation Agent, an Audit Agent, and a Memory Summarization Agent to iteratively build and verify software environments.
- The framework utilizes a propose-and-amplify strategy to generate large-scale, long-horizon tasks, which are then evaluated by a VLM Verifier using privileged information to ensure robust and granular performance assessment.
- By grounding software selection in U.S. GDP data, the authors construct CUA-World, a benchmark of over 10,000 tasks across 200 software applications, demonstrating that distillation and test-time auditing significantly improve agent performance on complex, long-horizon workflows.

---

[ACE-Bench: Agent Configurable Evaluation with Scalable Horizons and Controllable Difficulty under Lightweight Environments](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06111)

- ACE-Bench: introduces a lightweight benchmark for evaluating LLM-based agents using a unified grid-based planning task with configurable horizons and difficulty levels.
- The framework utilizes Static JSON Files to eliminate environment interaction overhead, enabling fast and reproducible evaluation across diverse domains.
- ACE-Bench provides fine-grained control through Scalable Horizons (hidden slots) and Controllable Difficulty (decoy budget) to systematically assess agent reasoning capabilities.

---

[Artificial Intelligence and the Structure of Mathematics](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06107)

- AMD framework: introduces a formal graph-theoretic representation of mathematics using Universal proof hypergraph (U) and Structural hypergraph (S) to model the autonomous discovery process of AI agents.
- The framework utilizes a generic discovery agent loop consisting of Goal generation agent, Prover agent, Learner agent, and Curation/compression agent to iteratively expand the mathematical corpus (Ct) through Reinforcement learning (RL) and LLM-components.
- This paper provides a set of criteria for evaluating autonomous mathematical discovery systems and speculates on the nature of human mathematics within the broader context of formal mathematical structures.

---

[Towards Securing IIoT: An Innovative Privacy-Preserving Anomaly Detector Based on Federated Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06101)

- DyHFL: introduces a privacy-preserving Federated Learning framework for IIoT anomaly detection that integrates Homomorphic Encryption and a dynamic agent selection scheme to mitigate straggler effects and communication bottlenecks.
- The framework utilizes a sliding window mechanism to continuously evaluate agent performance based on training time, communication latency, and local data size, ensuring fair participation across heterogeneous IIoT environments.
- DyHFL employs Weighted Average Metrics and Exponentially Weighted Moving Average functions to dynamically adjust selection thresholds, significantly improving convergence speed and model performance compared to traditional synchronous and asynchronous Federated Learning approaches.

---

[Social Dynamics as Critical Vulnerabilities that Undermine Objective Decision-Making in LLM Collectives](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06091)

- Representative-Centric Collective Decision-Making Framework: introduces a controlled multi-agent architecture to evaluate how social pressures, including social conformity, perceived expertise, dominant speaker effect, and rhetorical persuasion, undermine the objective decision-making of a central Representative Agent.
- The framework utilizes a Representative Agent that integrates diverse perspectives from a network of Peer Agents, where Adversarial Agents are specifically configured to manipulate the final judgment through biased rationales.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the Representative Agent's accuracy consistently degrades under social influence, highlighting critical vulnerabilities in LLM collectives that mirror human psychological biases.

---


[Distributed Algorithm for the Global Optimal Controller of Nonlinear Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05443)

- DOCA: introduces a distributed optimal control framework for nonlinear multi-agent systems that approximates the HJB equation using local information and neural networks.
- The framework utilizes a neural network-based numerical method to solve the HJB equation under privacy constraints where agents only share state and dynamic information with neighbors.
- Numerical simulations on a five-UGV formation demonstrate that the proposed distributed algorithm achieves superior performance compared to traditional consensus protocols.

---


[Coalitional Zero-Sum Games for H∞ Leader-Following Consensus Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06089)

- CZSG framework: introduces a robust leader-following consensus control strategy for multi-agent systems by formulating the interaction between controllers and adversarial attacks as a global coalitional min-max zero-sum game.
- The framework utilizes a decentralized computational strategy and a dynamic average consensus-based decoupling algorithm to resolve high-dimensional coupling, enabling distributed implementation of robust H∞ control laws.
- By decomposing the global generalized algebraic Riccati equation into local, low-dimensional versions, the approach ensures computational efficiency and scalability for large-scale networks under adversarial conditions.

---

[gyaradax: Local Gyrokinetics JAX Code](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06085)

- gyaradax: introduces a minimal, hardware-accelerated JAX solver for local flux-tube gyrokinetics, utilizing JAX, CUDA, and LLMs to bridge the gap between legacy Fortran codebases and modern ML workflows.
- The framework leverages coding agents and vibecoding to implement complex scientific computing components, including an RK4 integrator, cuFFT with LTO callbacks, and Z2Z packing for optimized performance.
- By employing mixed precision and custom CUDA kernels, the solver achieves significant speedups over traditional CPU-bound implementations while maintaining numerical parity with established benchmarks like GKW.

---

[From Hallucination to Structure Snowballing: The Alignment Tax of Constrained Decoding in LLM Reflection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06066)

- Logic-Guided Reflexion framework: introduces a ternary architecture consisting of an Actor, an Evaluator, and a Reflector that utilizes constrained decoding to enforce structured error attribution without additional training.
- The framework employs the Outlines library to translate a predefined Pydantic schema into a finite-state machine, which restricts token generation to ensure strict adherence to a 5-class error taxonomy.
- The study demonstrates that while constrained decoding mitigates hallucination snowballing, it introduces an "alignment tax" and a new failure mode termed "structure snowballing" where models become trapped in repetitive formatting loops.

---

[Incremental Risk Assessment for Cascading Failures in Large-Scale Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06024)

- Systemic Risk Assessment Framework: introduces a theoretical approach to quantify cascading failure risks in time-delay consensus networks using Average Value-at-Risk (AV@R) to model the propagation of large deviations.
- The framework derives closed-form expressions for conditional risk, linking network topology, communication delays, and noise statistics to systemic vulnerability.
- It establishes fundamental lower bounds on cascading risk and provides an efficient single-step update law for scalable real-time risk re-evaluation as new failures are detected.

---

[CritBench: A Framework for Evaluating Cybersecurity Capabilities of Large Language Models in IEC 61850 Digital Substation Environments](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06019)

- CritBench: introduces a systematic benchmarking framework designed to evaluate the cybersecurity capabilities of LLM agents within IEC 61850 Digital Substation environments.
- The framework utilizes a domain-specific tool scaffold, CritLayer, to enable LLMs to interact with industrial protocols and perform tasks ranging from static configuration analysis to dynamic system manipulation.
- Empirical evaluations across five state-of-the-art LLMs demonstrate that while models possess internalized knowledge for static analysis, they require specialized tooling to reliably navigate the complex, state-dependent requirements of industrial control systems.

---

[Epistemic Blinding: An Inference-Time Protocol for Auditing Prior Contamination in LLM-Assisted Analysis](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06013)

- Epistemic Blinding: introduces an inference-time protocol that mitigates parametric knowledge contamination in LLMs by anonymizing entity identifiers before reasoning, utilizing Data In, Anonymizer, LLM Agent, De-anonymizer, Scoring Function, and Claude Code Skill.
- The protocol restores auditability by comparing blinded and unblinded LLM outputs to measure the influence of training priors versus data-driven evidence.
- Experimental results in oncology and equity screening demonstrate that blinding systematically reduces fame bias, shifting rankings for 16-35% of top-20 candidates without degrading target recovery.

---

[Flowr — Scaling Up Retail Supply Chain Operations Through Agentic AI in Large Scale Supermarket Chains](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05987)

- Flowr: introduces a multi-agent framework for automating end-to-end retail supply chain workflows by decomposing operations into specialized agents coordinated by a human-in-the-loop orchestration model.
- The framework utilizes a consortium of fine-tuned, domain-specialized LLMs and a central reasoning LLM to ensure context-aware, explainable, and responsible decision-making across all supply chain stages.
- Each agentic workflow is exposed via a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, enabling a single human manager to supervise, validate, and intervene in automated processes through a unified interface.

---

[Adaptive Incentive Design with Regret Minimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05977)

- RAID (Regret-Minimizing Adaptive Incentive Design): introduces a framework for synthesizing incentive laws under information asymmetry by alternating between exploration and exploitation to minimize behavioral regret.
- The framework utilizes a switching policy that employs Gaussian probing to ensure strong consistency of the type estimator while regulating agent behavior toward a social optimum.
- By relaxing standard persistence-of-excitation assumptions through a weak diminishing-excitation condition, the approach achieves asymptotically vanishing regret in principal-agent Stackelberg games.

---

[A Formal Security Framework for MCP-Based AI Agents: Threat Taxonomy, Verification Models, and Defense Mechanisms](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05969)

- MCPSHIELD: introduces a comprehensive formal security framework for MCP-based AI agents, integrating L-CAC, L-CTA, L-IFT, and L-RPE to achieve 91% theoretical threat coverage.
- The framework provides a unified threat taxonomy of 7 categories and 23 attack vectors, alongside a labeled transition system for formal verification of security properties.
- MCPSHIELD addresses critical security gaps in the Model Context Protocol ecosystem by enforcing tool integrity, data confinement, privilege boundedness, and context isolation.

---

[FinReporting: An Agentic Workflow for Localized Reporting of Cross-Jurisdiction Financial Disclosures](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05966)

- FinReporting: introduces an agentic workflow for localized cross-jurisdiction financial reporting that utilizes Filing Acquisition, Statement Identification, Rule-based Extraction, Canonical Mapping, LLM Guardrail Layer, Conditional Expert Review Layer, Anomaly Log, and Audit Trail.
- The system replaces free-form LLM generation with a constrained verification-centered approach to ensure logical coherence and semantic faithfulness across heterogeneous accounting standards.
- By mapping diverse financial disclosures into a unified canonical ontology, the framework enables consistent cross-market benchmarking and auditable financial analysis.

---

[Does Pass Rate Tell the Whole Story? Evaluating Design Constraint Compliance in LLM-based Issue Resolution](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05955)

- SWE-Shield: introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLM-based issue resolution by making implicit project-specific design constraints explicit and measurable through DesignHunter, Satisfaction Verifier, and Human Judgers.
- The framework utilizes DesignHunter to extract design constraints from pull requests and a Satisfaction Verifier to assess whether generated patches comply with these constraints.
- Empirical results demonstrate that functional correctness is a poor proxy for design compliance, as many patches pass tests while violating project-specific design constraints.

---

[Towards Trustworthy Report Generation: A Deep Research Agent with Progressive Confidence Estimation and Calibration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05952)

- Deep Research Agent framework: introduces a modular system for trustworthy report generation that integrates progressive confidence estimation and calibration into a multi-stage pipeline.
- The system utilizes a Deliberative Search Model that performs iterative Think-Search-Read cycles to ground outputs in verifiable evidence while assigning confidence scores to individual claims.
- By decomposing complex research tasks into verifiable sub-queries, the framework enables fine-grained reliability assessment and enhances transparency in long-form report generation.

---

[MARL-GPT: Foundation Model for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05943)

- MARL-GPT: introduces a unified transformer-based architecture for multi-agent reinforcement learning that leverages imitation learning on expert trajectories to generalize across diverse environments using Observation Encoder, Transformer Blocks, Critic Decoder, Actor Head, Action Masking, and Positional Embeddings.
- The framework utilizes a structured observation encoding scheme that injects agent-specific, team-specific, and temporal information into tokens to enable permutation-invariant processing across heterogeneous multi-agent tasks.
- Empirical results demonstrate that the model achieves competitive performance against specialized baselines in SMACv2, Google Research Football, and POGEMA environments without requiring task-specific architectural modifications.

---

[Context-Value-Action Architecture for Value-Driven Large Language Model Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05939)

- CVA (Context-Value-Action) Architecture: introduces a decoupled "Generate-then-Verify" framework that separates action generation from cognitive reasoning to mitigate behavioral rigidity in LLMs.
- The framework utilizes a Value-Driven Verifier trained on authentic human data to evaluate candidate actions against dynamic value activations, replacing self-referential "LLM-as-a-judge" methods.
- The architecture is validated on CVABench, demonstrating superior behavioral fidelity and interpretability by effectively modeling human-like value-driven decision-making processes.

---

[Saliency-Guided Representation with Consistency Policy Learning for Visual Unsupervised Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05931)

- SRCP: introduces a framework that decouples representation learning from successor training to enhance zero-shot generalization in visual URL by focusing on dynamics-relevant features.
- The framework utilizes saliency-guided dynamics representation learning to improve successor measure estimation and a consistency-based policy with classifier-free guidance to model multi-modal skill-conditioned behaviors.
- SRCP demonstrates superior zero-shot generalization across 16 visual control tasks and remains compatible with various existing successor representation methods.

---

[Joint Knowledge Base Completion and Question Answering by Combining Large Language Models and Small Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05875)

- JCQL: introduces a framework that integrates LLM-based reasoning with SLM-based knowledge base completion to enable mutual enhancement between KBC and KBQA tasks.
- The framework utilizes an LLM agent for step-by-step reasoning and incorporates an SLM as an action to retrieve structured knowledge, while simultaneously fine-tuning the SLM using reasoning paths generated by the LLM.
- This iterative process leverages experience replay to mitigate catastrophic forgetting in the SLM, effectively combining parametric knowledge from LLMs with explicit structured knowledge from KBs.

---

[Deep Researcher Agent: An Autonomous Framework for 24/7 Deep Learning Experimentation with Zero-Cost Monitoring](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.05854)
